title: Efficacy of the pentavalent rotavirus vaccine, RotaTeq®, in Finnish infants up to 3 years of age: the Finnish Extension Study.
ehr_evidence: The study is a clinical trial (REST and FES) and does not mention the use of EHR or linked health data.
summary: The Finnish Extension Study (FES) evaluated the efficacy of the pentavalent rotavirus vaccine (RV5) in Finnish infants, following them for rotavirus-related hospitalizations and emergency department visits beyond the initial trial period.
key_findings: RV5 reduced RVGE hospitalizations and ED visits by 93.8% for up to 3.1 years.
RV5 had a substantial impact on all gastroenteritis-related hospitalizations and ED visits into the third year of life.
study_design: Double-blind, placebo-controlled, randomised trial with extension study
population: Finnish infants up to 3 years of age
limitations: Specific to a single vaccine and a specific geographic region.
